title: New Simple Surgical Procedure for Management of Endophthalmitis Phacoanaphylactic Glaucoma
author: Duong Dieu
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Phacolytic glaucoma (PG) was usually caused by the hypermature lens and the principal mechanism was the obstructed trabecular meshwork with the leaky lens capsule proteins. The PG with rupture crystalline capsule (anterior chamber was not observed) exactly endophthalmitis phacoanaphylactic glaucoma (EPG) is commonly misdiagnosed and was therefore refused lens extraction by local eye doctors at the district hospital level. Objective: To introduce a new simple surgical procedure lens extraction is easily performed at the district hospital for diagnosis as well as treatment.

keywords: Phacolytic glaucoma; endophthalmitis phacoanaphylactic glaucoma; nucleus lens extraction
